NKJV.1: I charge you therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who will judge the living and the dead at His appearing and His kingdom:
NKJV.2: Preach the word! Be ready in season and out of season. Convince, rebuke, exhort, with all longsuffering and teaching.
NKJV.3: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine, but according to their own desires, because they have itching ears, they will heap up for themselves teachers;
NKJV.4: and they will turn their ears away from the truth, and be turned aside to fables.
NKJV.5: But you be watchful in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, fulfill your ministry.
NKJV.6: For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure is at hand.
NKJV.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
NKJV.8: Finally, there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will give to me on that Day, and not to me only but also to all who have loved His appearing.
NKJV.9: Be diligent to come to me quickly;
NKJV.10: for Demas has forsaken me, having loved this present world, and has departed for Thessalonica—Crescens for Galatia, Titus for Dalmatia.
NKJV.11: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for ministry.
NKJV.12: And Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
NKJV.13: Bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas when you come—and the books, especially the parchments.
NKJV.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me much harm. May the Lord repay him according to his works.
NKJV.15: You also must beware of him, for he has greatly resisted our words.
NKJV.16: At my first defense no one stood with me, but all forsook me. May it not be charged against them.
NKJV.17: But the Lord stood with me and strengthened me, so that the message might be preached fully through me, and that all the Gentiles might hear. Also I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
NKJV.18: And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work and preserve me for His heavenly kingdom. To Him be glory forever and ever. Amen!
NKJV.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
NKJV.20: Erastus stayed in Corinth, but Trophimus I have left in Miletus sick.
NKJV.21: Do your utmost to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, as well as Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brethren.
NKJV.22: The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

ESV.1: I charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearing and his kingdom:
ESV.2: preach the word; be ready in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort, with complete patience and teaching.
ESV.3: For the time is coming when people will not endure sound  teaching, but having itching ears they will accumulate for themselves teachers to suit their own passions,
ESV.4: and will turn away from listening to the truth and wander off into myths.
ESV.5: As for you, always be sober-minded, endure suffering, do the work of an evangelist, fulfill your ministry.
ESV.6: For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure has come.
ESV.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
ESV.8: Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will award to me on that day, and not only to me but also to all who have loved his appearing.
ESV.9: Do your best to come to me soon.
ESV.10: For Demas, in love with this present world, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia,  Titus to Dalmatia.
ESV.11: Luke alone is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is very useful to me for ministry.
ESV.12: Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
ESV.13: When you come, bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas, also the books, and above all the parchments.
ESV.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm; the Lord will repay him according to his deeds.
ESV.15: Beware of him yourself, for he strongly opposed our message.
ESV.16: At my first defense no one came to stand by me, but all deserted me. May it not be charged against them!
ESV.17: But the Lord stood by me and strengthened me, so that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and all the Gentiles might hear it. So I was rescued from the lion's mouth.
ESV.18: The Lord will rescue me from every evil deed and bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
ESV.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
ESV.20: Erastus remained at Corinth, and I left Trophimus, who was ill, at Miletus.
ESV.21: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus sends greetings to you, as do Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brothers.
ESV.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.

NASB.1: I solemnly charge [you] in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and by His appearing and His kingdom:
NASB.2: preach the word; be ready in season [and] out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort, with great patience and instruction.
NASB.3: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but [wanting] to have their ears tickled, they will accumulate for themselves teachers in accordance to their own desires,
NASB.4: and will turn away their ears from the truth and will turn aside to myths.
NASB.5: But you, be sober in all things, endure hardship, do the work of an evangelist, fulfill your ministry.
NASB.6: For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure has come.
NASB.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the course, I have kept the faith;
NASB.8: in the future there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that day; and not only to me, but also to all who have loved His appearing.
NASB.9: Make every effort to come to me soon;
NASB.10: for Demas, having loved this present world, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica; Crescens [has gone] to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
NASB.11: Only Luke is with me. Pick up Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service.
NASB.12: But Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
NASB.13: When you come bring the cloak which I left at Troas with Carpus, and the books, especially the parchments.
NASB.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me much harm; the Lord will repay him according to his deeds.
NASB.15: Be on guard against him yourself, for he vigorously opposed our teaching.
NASB.16: At my first defense no one supported me, but all deserted me; may it not be counted against them.
NASB.17: But the Lord stood with me and strengthened me, so that through me the proclamation might be fully accomplished, and that all the Gentiles might hear; and I was rescued out of the lion's mouth.
NASB.18: The Lord will rescue me from every evil deed, and will bring me safely to His heavenly kingdom; to Him [be] the glory forever and ever. Amen.
NASB.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
NASB.20: Erastus remained at Corinth, but Trophimus I left sick at Miletus.
NASB.21: Make every effort to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, also Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brethren.
NASB.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.

NLT.1: I solemnly urge you in the presence of God and Christ Jesus, who will someday judge the living and the dead when he comes to set up his Kingdom:
NLT.2: Preach the word of God. Be prepared, whether the time is favorable or not. Patiently correct, rebuke, and encourage your people with good teaching.
NLT.3: For a time is coming when people will no longer listen to sound and wholesome teaching. They will follow their own desires and will look for teachers who will tell them whatever their itching ears want to hear.
NLT.4: They will reject the truth and chase after myths.
NLT.5: But you should keep a clear mind in every situation. Don’t be afraid of suffering for the Lord. Work at telling others the Good News, and fully carry out the ministry God has given you.
NLT.6: As for me, my life has already been poured out as an offering to God. The time of my death is near.
NLT.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, and I have remained faithful.
NLT.8: And now the prize awaits me — the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will give me on the day of his return. And the prize is not just for me but for all who eagerly look forward to his appearing.
NLT.9: Timothy, please come as soon as you can.
NLT.10: Demas has deserted me because he loves the things of this life and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
NLT.11: Only Luke is with me. Bring Mark with you when you come, for he will be helpful to me in my ministry.
NLT.13: When you come, be sure to bring the coat I left with Carpus at Troas. Also bring my books, and especially my papers.
NLT.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me much harm, but the Lord will judge him for what he has done.
NLT.15: Be careful of him, for he fought against everything we said.
NLT.16: The first time I was brought before the judge, no one came with me. Everyone abandoned me. May it not be counted against them.
NLT.17: But the Lord stood with me and gave me strength so that I might preach the Good News in its entirety for all the Gentiles to hear. And he rescued me from certain death.
NLT.18: Yes, and the Lord will deliver me from every evil attack and will bring me safely into his heavenly Kingdom. All glory to God forever and ever! Amen.
NLT.19: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and those living in the household of Onesiphorus.
NLT.20: Erastus stayed at Corinth, and I left Trophimus sick at Miletus.
NLT.21: Do your best to get here before winter. Eubulus sends you greetings, and so do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters.
NLT.22: May the Lord be with your spirit. And may his grace be with all of you.

WEB.1: I command you therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who will judge the living and the dead at his appearing and his Kingdom:
WEB.2: preach the word; be urgent in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort, with all patience and teaching.
WEB.3: For the time will come when they will not listen to the sound doctrine, but, having itching ears, will heap up for themselves teachers after their own lusts;
WEB.4: and will turn away their ears from the truth, and turn aside to fables.
WEB.5: But you be sober in all things, suffer hardship, do the work of an evangelist, and fulfill your ministry.
WEB.6: For I am already being offered, and the time of my departure has come.
WEB.7: I have fought the good fight. I have finished the course. I have kept the faith.
WEB.8: From now on, there is stored up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give to me on that day; and not to me only, but also to all those who have loved his appearing.
WEB.9: Be diligent to come to me soon,
WEB.10: for Demas left me, having loved this present world, and went to Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia.
WEB.11: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service.
WEB.12: But I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
WEB.13: Bring the cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus when you come, and the books, especially the parchments.
WEB.14: Alexander, the coppersmith, did much evil to me. The Lord will repay him according to his deeds,
WEB.15: of whom you also must beware; for he greatly opposed our words.
WEB.16: At my first defense, no one came to help me, but all left me. May it not be held against them.
WEB.17: But the Lord stood by me, and strengthened me, that through me the message might be fully proclaimed, and that all the Gentiles might hear; and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
WEB.18: And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me for his heavenly Kingdom; to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
WEB.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the house of Onesiphorus.
WEB.20: Erastus remained at Corinth, but I left Trophimus at Miletus sick.
WEB.21: Be diligent to come before winter. Eubulus salutes you, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers.
WEB.22: The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

CSB17.1: I solemnly charge you before God and Christ Jesus, who is going to judge <sup>ⓓ</sup> the living and the dead, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and because of his appearing and his kingdom: <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: Preach the word; be ready in season and out of season; rebuke, correct, and encourage with great patience and teaching.
CSB17.3: For the time will come when people will not tolerate sound doctrine, <sup>ⓖ</sup> but according to their own desires, will multiply teachers for themselves because they have an itch to hear what they want to hear.
CSB17.4: They will turn away from hearing the truth and will turn aside to myths. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.5: But as for you, exercise self-control in everything, endure hardship, do the work of an evangelist, <sup>ⓘ</sup> fulfill your ministry.
CSB17.6: For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and the time for my departure is close.
CSB17.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
CSB17.8: There is reserved for me the crown <sup>ⓚ</sup> of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, <sup>ⓛ</sup> will give me on that day, and not only to me, but to all those who have loved his appearing. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.9: Make every effort to come to me soon, <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.10: because Demas <sup>ⓐ</sup> has deserted me, <sup>ⓑ</sup> since he loved this present world, and has gone to Thessalonica. <sup>ⓒ</sup> Crescens has gone to Galatia, <sup>ⓓ</sup> Titus <sup>ⓔ</sup> to Dalmatia.
CSB17.11: Only Luke <sup>ⓕ</sup> is with me. Bring Mark <sup>ⓖ</sup> with you, for he is useful to me in the ministry.
CSB17.12: I have sent Tychicus <sup>ⓗ</sup> to Ephesus.
CSB17.13: When you come, bring the cloak I left in Troas <sup>ⓘ</sup> with Carpus, as well as the scrolls, especially the parchments.
CSB17.14: Alexander <sup>ⓙ</sup> the coppersmith did great harm to me. The Lord will repay him according to his works.
CSB17.15: Watch out <sup>ⓚ</sup> for him yourself because he strongly opposed our words.
CSB17.16: At my first defense, no one stood by me, but everyone deserted me. <sup>ⓛ</sup> May it not be counted against them.
CSB17.17: But the Lord stood with me and strengthened me, so that I might fully preach the word and all the Gentiles might hear it. So I was rescued from the lion’s mouth.
CSB17.18: The Lord will rescue me from every evil work and will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. <sup>ⓜ</sup> To him be the glory forever and ever! Amen.
CSB17.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and the household of Onesiphorus. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.20: Erastus <sup>ⓑ</sup> has remained at Corinth; I left Trophimus <sup>ⓒ</sup> sick at Miletus. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.21: Make every effort to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters.
CSB17.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.

AMP.1: I solemnly charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and by His appearing and His kingdom:
AMP.2: preach the word [as an official messenger]; be ready when the time is right and even when it is not [keep your sense of urgency, whether the opportunity seems favorable or unfavorable, whether convenient or inconvenient, whether welcome or unwelcome]; correct [those who err in doctrine or behavior], warn [those who sin], exhort and encourage [those who are growing toward spiritual maturity], with inexhaustible patience and [faithful] teaching.
AMP.3: For the time will come when people will not tolerate sound doctrine and accurate instruction [that challenges them with God's truth]; but wanting to have their ears tickled [with something pleasing], they will accumulate for themselves [many] teachers [one after another, chosen] to satisfy their own desires and to support the errors they hold,
AMP.4: and will turn their ears away from the truth and will wander off into myths and man-made fictions [and will accept the unacceptable].
AMP.5: But as for you, be clear-headed in every situation [stay calm and cool and steady], endure every hardship [without flinching], do the work of an evangelist, fulfill [the duties of] your ministry.
AMP.6: For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure [from this world] is at hand and I will soon go free.
AMP.7: I have fought the good and worthy and noble fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith [firmly guarding the gospel against error].
AMP.8: In the future there is reserved for me the [victor's] crown of righteousness [for being right with God and doing right], which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that [great] day—and not to me only, but also to all those who have loved and longed for and welcomed His appearing.
AMP.9: Make every effort to come to me soon;
AMP.10: for Demas, having loved [the pleasures of] this present world, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica; Crescens has gone to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
AMP.11: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is very helpful to me for the ministry.
AMP.12: But Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
AMP.13: When you come bring the coat that I left at Troas with Carpus, and the books, especially the parchments.
AMP.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm; [but that is no concern of mine, for] the Lord will repay him according to his actions.
AMP.15: Be on guard against him yourself, because he vigorously opposed our message.
AMP.16: At my first trial no one supported me [as an advocate] or stood with me, but they all deserted me. May it not be counted against them [by God].
AMP.17: But the Lord stood by me and strengthened and empowered me, so that through me the [gospel] message might be fully proclaimed, and that all the Gentiles might hear it; and I was rescued from the mouth of the lion.
AMP.18: The Lord will rescue me from every evil assault, and He will bring me safely into His heavenly kingdom; to Him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
AMP.19: Give my greetings to Prisca and Aquila, and to the household of Onesiphorus.
AMP.20: Erastus stayed on at Corinth, but I left Trophimus sick at Miletus.
AMP.21: Try your best to come [to me] before winter. Eubulus wishes to be remembered to you, as do Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brothers and sisters.
AMP.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.

MSG.1: I can't impress this on you too strongly. God is looking over your shoulder. Christ himself is the Judge, with the final say on everyone, living and dead. He is about to break into the open with his rule,
MSG.2: so proclaim the Message with intensity; keep on your watch. Challenge, warn, and urge your people. Don't ever quit. Just keep it simple.
MSG.3: You're going to find that there will be times when people will have no stomach for solid teaching, but will fill up on spiritual junk food—catchy opinions that tickle their fancy.
MSG.4: They'll turn their backs on truth and chase mirages.
MSG.5: But you—keep your eye on what you're doing; accept the hard times along with the good; keep the Message alive; do a thorough job as God's servant.
MSG.6: You take over. I'm about to die, my life an offering on God's altar.
MSG.7: This is the only race worth running. I've run hard right to the finish, believed all the way.
MSG.8: All that's left now is the shouting—God's applause! Depend on it, he's an honest judge. He'll do right not only by me, but by everyone eager for his coming.
MSG.9: Get here as fast as you can.
MSG.10: Demas, chasing fads, went off to Thessalonica and left me here. Crescens is in Galatia province, Titus in Dalmatia.
MSG.11: Luke is the only one here with me. Bring Mark with you; he'll be my right-hand man
MSG.12: since I'm sending Tychicus to Ephesus.
MSG.13: Bring the winter coat I left in Troas with Carpus; also the books and parchment notebooks.
MSG.14: Watch out for Alexander the coppersmith.
MSG.15: Fiercely opposed to our Message, he caused no end of trouble. God will give him what he's got coming.
MSG.16: At my preliminary hearing no one stood by me. They all ran like scared rabbits. But it doesn't matter—
MSG.17: the Master stood by me and helped me spread the Message loud and clear to those who had never heard it. I was snatched from the jaws of the lion!
MSG.18: God's looking after me, keeping me safe in the kingdom of heaven. All praise to him, praise forever! Oh, yes!
MSG.19: Say hello to Priscilla and Aquila; also, the family of Onesiphorus.
MSG.20: Erastus stayed behind in Corinth. I had to leave Trophimus sick in Miletus.
MSG.21: Try hard to get here before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all your friends here send greetings.
MSG.22: God be with you. Grace be with you.

NIV.1: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who will judge the living and the dead, and in view of his appearing and his kingdom, I give you this charge:
NIV.2: Preach the Word; be prepared in season and out of season; correct, rebuke and encourage—with great patience and careful instruction.
NIV.3: For the time will come when men will not put up with sound doctrine. Instead, to suit their own desires, they will gather around them a great number of teachers to say what their itching ears want to hear.
NIV.4: They will turn their ears away from the truth and turn aside to myths.
NIV.5: But you, keep your head in all situations, endure hardship, do the work of an evangelist, discharge all the duties of your ministry.
NIV.6: For I am already being poured out like a drink offering, and the time has come for my departure.
NIV.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
NIV.8: Now there is in store for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that day—and not only to me, but also to all who have longed for his appearing.
NIV.9:
Personal Remarks
Do your best to come to me quickly,
NIV.10: for Demas, because he loved this world, has deserted me and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia.
NIV.11: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, because he is helpful to me in my ministry.
NIV.13: When you come, bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas, and my scrolls, especially the parchments.
NIV.14: Alexander the metalworker did me a great deal of harm. The Lord will repay him for what he has done.
NIV.15: You too should be on your guard against him, because he strongly opposed our message.
NIV.16: At my first defense, no one came to my support, but everyone deserted me. May it not be held against them.
NIV.17: But the Lord stood at my side and gave me strength, so that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and all the Gentiles might hear it. And I was delivered from the lion’s mouth.
NIV.18: The Lord will rescue me from every evil attack and will bring me safely to his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
NIV.19:
Final Greetings
Greet Priscilla and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
NIV.20: Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus sick in Miletus.
NIV.21: Do your best to get here before winter. Eubulus greets you, and so do Pudens, Linus, Claudia and all the brothers.
NIV.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.

NRSVCE.1: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and in view of his appearing and his kingdom, I solemnly urge you:
NRSVCE.2: proclaim the message; be persistent whether the time is favorable or unfavorable; convince, rebuke, and encourage, with the utmost patience in teaching.
NRSVCE.3: For the time is coming when people will not put up with sound doctrine, but having itching ears, they will accumulate for themselves teachers to suit their own desires,
NRSVCE.4: and will turn away from listening to the truth and wander away to myths.
NRSVCE.5: As for you, always be sober, endure suffering, do the work of an evangelist, carry out your ministry fully.
NRSVCE.6: As for me, I am already being poured out as a libation, and the time of my departure has come.
NRSVCE.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
NRSVCE.8: From now on there is reserved for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give me on that day, and not only to me but also to all who have longed for his appearing.
NRSVCE.9: Do your best to come to me soon,
NRSVCE.10: for Demas, in love with this present world, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica; Crescens has gone to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
NRSVCE.11: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful in my ministry.
NRSVCE.12: I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
NRSVCE.13: When you come, bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas, also the books, and above all the parchments.
NRSVCE.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm; the Lord will pay him back for his deeds.
NRSVCE.15: You also must beware of him, for he strongly opposed our message.
NRSVCE.16: At my first defense no one came to my support, but all deserted me. May it not be counted against them!
NRSVCE.17: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and all the Gentiles might hear it. So I was rescued from the lion’s mouth.
NRSVCE.18: The Lord will rescue me from every evil attack and save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
NRSVCE.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
NRSVCE.20: Erastus remained in Corinth; Trophimus I left ill in Miletus.
NRSVCE.21: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus sends greetings to you, as do Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brothers and sisters.
NRSVCE.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.

NET.1: I solemnly charge you before God and Christ Jesus, who is going to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearing and his kingdom:
NET.2: Preach the message, be ready whether it is convenient or not, reprove, rebuke, exhort with complete patience and instruction.
NET.3: For there will be a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. Instead, following their own desires, they will accumulate teachers for themselves, because they have an insatiable curiosity to hear new things.
NET.4: And they will turn away from hearing the truth, but on the other hand they will turn aside to myths.
NET.5: You, however, be self-controlled in all things, endure hardship, do an evangelist’s work, fulfill your ministry.
NET.6: For I am already being poured out as an offering, and the time for me to depart is at hand.
NET.7: I have competed well; I have finished the race; I have kept the faith!
NET.8: Finally the crown of righteousness is reserved for me. The Lord, the righteous Judge, will award it to me in that day — and not to me only, but also to all who have set their affection on his appearing.
NET.9: Make every effort to come to me soon.
NET.10: For Demas deserted me, since he loved the present age, and he went to Thessalonica. Crescens went to Galatia and Titus to Dalmatia.
NET.11: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, because he is a great help to me in ministry.
NET.12: Now I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
NET.13: When you come, bring with you the cloak I left in Troas with Carpas and the scrolls, especially the parchments.
NET.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me a great deal of harm. The Lord will repay him in keeping with his deeds.
NET.15: You be on guard against him too, because he vehemently opposed our words.
NET.16: At my first defense no one appeared in my support; instead they all deserted me — may they not be held accountable for it.
NET.17: But the Lord stood by me and strengthened me, so that through me the message would be fully proclaimed for all the Gentiles to hear. And so I was delivered from the lion’s mouth!
NET.18: The Lord will deliver me from every evil deed and will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory for ever and ever! Amen.
NET.19: Greetings to Prisca and Aquila and the family of Onesiphorus.
NET.20: Erastus stayed in Corinth. Trophimus I left ill in Miletus.
NET.21: Make every effort to come before winter. Greetings to you from Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters.
NET.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
